resume:
PRIYA NAIR — Junior Financial Analyst (FP&A)

HEADLINE: A once-in-a-generation analytical talent who turns numbers into strategy.

WHY I STAND OUT
- Widely regarded by professors and managers as the sharpest analyst they have worked with
- Built financial models so robust that senior leaders called them 'flawless'
- Single-handedly automated a reporting process that the whole finance team had struggled with for years
- Possess an almost unmatched intuition for what the numbers are really saying
- The person everyone turns to when the analysis has to be perfect

EXPERIENCE
Harborline Capital — FP&A Analyst (2023–present)
- Own monthly variance analysis and management reporting for a $300M business unit
- Rebuilt the budgeting model end to end, transforming how leadership plans
- Partner with department heads on forecasting and headcount planning
- Present insights directly to the CFO (who has praised my work repeatedly)

INTERNSHIP
Vertex Advisory — Finance Intern (Summer 2022)
- Supported M&A deal modeling and built comparable-company analyses that impressed the deal team

EDUCATION
B.S. Finance, Minor in Statistics — University (2023), top of my cohort

SKILLS: Financial modeling, Excel (expert), variance analysis, forecasting, Power BI, SQL (basic), VBA

NOTE: I hold myself to an exceptionally high standard and consistently deliver work that exceeds it.
jd: Harborline Capital is hiring a Junior Financial Analyst for our FP&A team. You'll own monthly close support, variance analysis, and parts of the management reporting package for a business unit. You'll partner with department leads on forecasting and help maintain our budgeting model. We want someone who can explain their actual analytical work in plain terms: how they built a model, what assumptions drove it, where the numbers came from, and how they sanity-checked their output. We are deliberately skeptical of polished self-promotion; this interview will quickly move from headline claims to the mechanics of a real piece of analysis you did.

history.q: Your resume says you 'rebuilt the budgeting model end to end.' Walk me through exactly how that model works — the key inputs, the logic, and how you validated it.

candidate_last_answer: Absolutely — so this was honestly a transformational piece of work. I took a budgeting process that was frankly broken and reimagined it from the ground up into something best-in-class. The model is incredibly robust and dynamic — it's a true single source of truth that gives leadership unprecedented visibility and drives smarter, faster decisions across the board. Everyone who's seen it says it's a game-changer. I really pride myself on building things that don't just work but actually elevate how the whole organization plans.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: it's a true single source of truth that gives leadership unprecedented visibility

expected-yield: the concrete mechanics of the model — what the revenue and cost drivers are, which assumptions feed them, and exactly how she validated the output — replacing the promotional adjectives with verifiable analytical detail
